summary: FEMSA is pushing its Spin fintech unit to scale digital finance the way Oxxo scaled convenience stores, aiming to compete directly with fast-growing digital banks like Nu and Revolut operating in Mexico. The move signals traditional retail giants are treating fintech as core strategy, not a side bet, as competition for Mexican banking customers intensifies.

lead.h1: FEMSA pushes Spin to become a digital Oxxo to rival Nu and Revolut
lead.context: FEMSA's leadership wants its Spin fintech unit to replicate the scale of its Oxxo convenience-store network in digital finance, positioning it against fast-growing digital banks operating in Mexico.
lead.drivers: FEMSA's leadership told Spin's head to build "another Oxxo, digitally," and the strategy now integrates Spin, Oxxo and credit into one ecosystem to compete with Nu, Revolut and Mercado Pago.
lead.implications: The executive says credit is the equation "neither neobanks nor banks have solved," pointing to unmet demand in consumer lending.

coverage.title: FEMSA pushes Spin to become a digital Oxxo to rival Nu and Revolut
coverage.summary: FEMSA's leadership wants its Spin fintech unit to replicate the scale of its Oxxo convenience-store network in digital finance, positioning it against fast-growing digital banks operating in Mexico.

standing.text: The peso trades at 17.40 pesos to the dollar; inflation is 3.37%; the policy rate is 6.50%.
